channel.title: Code Defence Intelligence Brief — Mon, 30 Mar 2026
channel.description: Daily cybersecurity intelligence for Mon, 30 Mar 2026.

item.title: New macOS Infinity Stealer uses fake CAPTCHAs to bypass security. 🛡️
item.description:
A sophisticated new infostealer is targeting macOS users with highly deceptive delivery techniques. 🛡️
Threat Alert · Infinity Stealer leveraging fake Cloudflare CAPTCHAs and ClickFix.
Researchers have identified Infinity Stealer‚ a macOS-specific malware that targets browser data‚ cookies‚ and cryptocurrency wallets. The malware is delivered via fake Cloudflare verification pages that trick users into running a malicious command in the terminal. The payload is a Python-based executable compiled with Nuitka‚ making it significantly harder for traditional antivirus tools to detect.
This marks the first documented macOS campaign combining ClickFix delivery with a Python-based infostealer. Attackers are increasingly targeting macOS as enterprise adoption grows‚ specifically aiming for high-value session tokens that can bypass multi-factor authentication.
The uncomfortable truth: Your users trust in security verification symbols like CAPTCHAs is being weaponized to trick them into executing terminal commands that dismantle your endpoint security.
→ Educate users to never copy and paste commands into the Terminal from untrusted or unverified websites. → Use EDR tools to monitor for unusual shell activity originating from browser processes on @[Apple](urn:li:organization:162479) devices. → Block known malicious domains associated with the Infinity Stealer infrastructure.
#Cybersecurity #macOS #Infostealer #SocialEngineering #EDR #CodeDefence

item.title: LangChain path traversal flaw exposes sensitive AI pipeline secrets. 🤖
item.guid:
https://codedefence.in/blog/2026/03/30/langchain-path-traversal-bug-ai-pipeline-risk HIGH CVE-2026-34070
A critical input validation flaw in a popular AI framework allows for arbitrary file reads on host systems. 🤖
CVE-2026-34070 · Severity 7.5 · Path Traversal in LangChain Template Loader.
Security researchers have disclosed a path traversal vulnerability in LangChain. The flaw arises from how the framework resolves file paths when loading prompt templates or external resources. By supplying crafted input‚ an unauthenticated attacker can traverse directories and read arbitrary files from the host system‚ potentially exposing configuration files and API credentials.
This is the latest in a series of vulnerabilities targeting AI orchestration tools. As these frameworks gain more autonomous access to internal systems‚ a single input validation bug can lead to the full compromise of the underlying application environment and its connected data sources.
The uncomfortable truth: The speed of AI adoption is creating significant technical debt in input sanitization‚ turning your newest productivity tools into your most accessible backdoors.
→ Update LangChain to the latest version immediately to resolve CVE-2026-34070. → Implement strict input validation and sanitization for all user-supplied data entering AI prompts or templates. → Run AI orchestration agents in isolated‚ low-privilege containers with restricted access to the local filesystem.
#Cybersecurity #AISecurity #LangChain #AppSec #VulnerabilityManagement #CodeDefence

item.title: CISA adds Aqua Security Trivy supply chain flaw to KEV catalog. 🛑
item.description:
The automated security scanner you trust to find vulnerabilities was itself weaponized in a global campaign. 🛑
CVE-2026-33634 · Severity Critical · CISA KEV Addition for Trivy Supply Chain Breach.
The @[CISA](urn:li:organization:13010360) has added a critical vulnerability impacting the Trivy vulnerability scanner to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. This follows the recent supply chain attack by the TeamPCP group‚ where malicious code was injected into the official release tags on GitHub to siphon CI/CD secrets and cloud credentials.
This addition underscores the extreme risk posed by supply chain compromises of core security tools. Federal agencies and private organizations are urged to verify the integrity of their scanning pipelines and assume compromise if vulnerable versions were in use during the last 14 days.
The uncomfortable truth: When your vulnerability scanner is compromised‚ the very tool you use to manage risk becomes the primary vehicle for high-velocity data exfiltration.
→ Audit your CI/CD pipelines to ensure you are using verified‚ non-malicious versions of Trivy. → Immediately rotate all secrets‚ tokens‚ and API keys that were present on any system where a compromised version of Trivy was executed. → Move to pin-to-hash policies for all third-party GitHub Actions to prevent future tag-hijacking attacks.
#Cybersecurity #SupplyChain #DevSecOps #Trivy #CISA #CodeDefence

item.title: Citrix NetScaler systems probed ahead of suspected CVE-2026-3055 exploitation. 🐚
item.description:
Attackers are actively scanning for vulnerable gateways to leak sensitive session tokens. 🐚
CVE-2026-3055 · Severity 9.3 · Active Reconnaissance for NetScaler Memory Overread.
Cybersecurity researchers are warning of a massive surge in reconnaissance activity targeting @[Citrix](urn:li:organization:1598) NetScaler ADC and Gateway appliances. Attackers are probing for CVE-2026-3055‚ a critical memory overread flaw that allows an unauthenticated attacker to leak potentially sensitive information from the application process memory.
Leaked data can include session tokens and administrative credentials‚ which can be used to bypass multi-factor authentication. This probing phase is often the precursor to a wide-scale automated exploitation campaign targeting enterprise remote access infrastructure.
The uncomfortable truth: Your perimeter is being mapped in real-time by adversaries looking for the single unpatched gateway that grants them access to your entire network.
→ Update NetScaler ADC and Gateway to the latest patched releases immediately. → Monitor your gateway logs for unusual scanning patterns and failed authentication attempts from non-standard IP ranges. → Implement strict egress filtering on your gateway appliances to limit the impact of potential session data leaks.
#Cybersecurity #NetworkSecurity #Citrix #PatchManagement #SOC #CodeDefence
